title: H2APEX and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners Enter Into Strategic Partnership for Pioneering Hydrogen Project in Lubmin
content: H2APEX Group SCA announces that Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) â€“ one of the worldâ€™s leading investment companies in energy infrastructure investments â€“ has acquired a majority stake of 70% in the first phase of H2APEXâ€™s IPCEI-funded hydrogen project in Lubmin through its Energy Transition Fund (CI ETF I) as a strategic investor. The aim of the contractual agreement is to secure pre-financing of the European Union (EU) IPCEI funding of EUR 167 million and to raise the additional financial resources required. Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners will invest EUR 15 million in the project phase until the final investment decision is made.
author: Analysts Account
